sections.paragraph:
συναναπαύομαι 1 aor. συνανεπαυσάμην to relax in someone’s company, rest with τινί someone Ro 15:32 (perh. w. suggestion of relaxation after the public service, διακονία, described vs. 31, cp. ἀνάπαυσις λειτουργίας PFlor 57, 56—Elsewh. only lit.=‘lie down, sleep w. somebody’ [Arrian, Cyneg. 9, 2], esp. of married couples [Dionys. Hal.; Plut.].—Is 11:6).—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

perh. perh. = perhaps

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

PFlor PFlor = Papiri Fiorentini—List 4

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

esp. esp. = especially

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 965
